steps.title: Look for BBC influence
steps.content: The student's claim is that the Corporation for Public Broadcasting and NPR were inspired by the BBC. Strong evidence must do more than mention that the BBC came first; it must connect the BBC's purpose to the thinking of US legislators who supported public broadcasting.

steps.title: Separate weak evidence
steps.content: A only describes why British politicians made the BBC a public company. B contrasts the BBC's lack of competition with NPR's competitive broadcasting market, which shows a difference rather than inspiration. C explains US politicians' general belief that government could enrich people's lives, but it does not mention the BBC.

steps.title: Choose the direct link
steps.content: The strongest quotation states that the BBC aimed to support democracy by promoting an informed citizenry and that US legislators believed access to high-quality programming could do the same for democracy in the United States. That directly links the BBC model to the US public broadcasting goal. The answer is D.
